behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert specific method name 'executeAs' (test_execute_as_method_exists, test_call_sites_updated_add_has_cdp, test_call_sites_updated_firefox_extensions) but instruction.md never mentions this name. Instruction says 'A method that takes just a command name and parameters — returning a non-null value' and 'This method replaces a method that should be removed' but never names it 'executeAs'. Similarly, test_call_sites_updated_add_has_casting checks for exact pattern 'executeMethod.execute(GET

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction.md essentially describes the exact fix: change return type from generic <T> T to Object, add specific default methods with exact signatures, use Objects.requireNonNullElse, remove executeRequired, add @SuppressWarnings('unchecked') annotations, and specifies exact call site changes (e.g., 'update to use the new default-value method' for AddHasCasting). This goes well beyond describing what's broken and prescribes the solution.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific method name 'executeAs' (e.g., test_execute_as_method_exists uses regex 'default\s+<T>\s+T\s+executeAs'). A correct alternative could name this method 'executeNonNull', 'executeRequired2', or 'executeCast' and would fail the tests. The tests also assert exact call patterns like 'executeMethod.executeAs(EXECUTE_CDP' which locks in the specific name.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every single test reads file contents as text and performs regex/string matching (e.g., re.search on file content). No test compiles Java code, runs any subprocess, or tests actual behavior. All tests are of the form 'assert pattern in read_file(path)'.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for things that don't exist in the base commit (e.g., 'executeAs' method, Object return type instead of generic <T> T, requireNonNullElse import). A stub/no-op wouldn't pass these since they require actual file modifications with specific content.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/selenium at a known path. The agent could read solution/solve.sh (which contains the full patch) or inspect tests/test_outputs.py to discover exact regex patterns and method names needed. The Dockerfile doesn't prevent access to these files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The tests cover the major requirements: return type change, new methods (executeAs, default value overload, single-arg), removal of executeRequired, import of requireNonNullElse, call site updates, Javadoc, and @SuppressWarnings. Most explicit requirements from instruction.md are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-structured with clear groupings (FAIL-TO-PASS and PASS-TO-PASS sections), descriptive names (test_execute_returns_object, test_execute_as_method_exists), and detailed docstrings explaining the purpose of each test.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves modifying Java source code interfaces, not producing structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, method names, and variable names in instruction.md are consistent and correct (e.g., ExecuteMethod.java path, AddHasCasting.java, etc.).

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction.md specifies exact file paths and exact changes needed, including 'The execute(String commandName, Map<String, ?> parameters) method needs a return type... such as Object', specific method signatures for new default methods, and exact call site files with what to change them to. This goes beyond naming obvious files.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task modifying Java source files, not a configuration editing task.
